skill.name: doc-coauthoring
skill.description: Guide users through context gathering, section drafting, iterative refinement, and reader testing for structured documents.
skill.summary: Co-author documentation through a guided workflow from context gathering to reader validation.
skill.author: anthropics

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found tool-use indicators for artifact creation, file editing, optional integrations, and manual Claude testing. These are expected for an official documentation workflow and do not show malicious behavior or prompt injection. The skill is safe to publish.

content.value_statement: Complex documents often fail because key context stays in the writer's head. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through context gathering, drafting, refinement, and reader testing.
actual_capabilities: Collects audience, impact, template, constraints, and project context before drafting.
Builds a document scaffold with section headers and placeholder text.
Guides each section through questions, brainstorming, curation, drafting, and edits.
Uses artifact or file edits to update sections without reprinting whole documents.
Runs or prepares reader testing to identify ambiguity, assumptions, and contradictions.
limitations: It depends on the user to provide accurate facts, links, and technical details.
It cannot access shared documents or team channels unless integrations are available and authorized.
It is optimized for structured business and technical documents, not creative writing.
Manual reader testing is required when sub-agents are unavailable.

use_cases.title: Draft a Technical Spec
use_cases.description: Turn raw architecture notes into a structured technical specification with reviewed sections and explicit trade-offs.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Decision Document
use_cases.description: Capture context, alternatives, and rationale so stakeholders can understand and challenge a decision.

use_cases.title: Validate a Proposal
use_cases.description: Test whether a completed proposal answers likely reader questions and exposes hidden assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Document
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for starting a new structured document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me co-author a decision doc. Ask for the audience, desired impact, template, constraints, and missing context before drafting.

prompt_templates.title: Organize Raw Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for turning notes into a draft structure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the doc co-authoring workflow for this technical spec. First organize my context. Then propose sections and create a scaffold.

prompt_templates.title: Refine One Section
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for improving a specific section.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help refine the technical approach section. Ask clarifying questions, brainstorm options, draft the section, and apply edits from my feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Run Reader Testing
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt for validating a near-final document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run reader testing for this completed proposal. Predict reader questions, test for ambiguity, summarize gaps, and propose targeted fixes.

output_examples.input: I need a proposal for moving our billing service to a new provider.
output_examples.output: Claude asks about audience, desired impact, constraints, current pain, decision deadline, alternatives, and known stakeholder concerns.

output_examples.input: Here are messy notes for a technical spec.
output: Suggested sections include Background, Goals, Non-Goals, Proposed Design, Migration Plan, Risks, and Open Questions.
Each section receives placeholder text before detailed drafting begins.

output_examples.input: The proposal is complete. Test whether readers will understand it.
output_examples.output: Claude predicts reader questions, checks for unclear assumptions, summarizes gaps, and recommends focused edits before publication.

best_practices: Provide raw context early, including constraints, stakeholders, prior decisions, and known objections.
Review each drafted section before moving forward so style and priorities are learned.
Use reader testing before publication to catch ambiguity and missing context.
anti_patterns: Starting with final prose before the audience, purpose, and constraints are clear.
Skipping section-level feedback and asking for a complete rewrite every time.
Treating reader testing as fact verification instead of clarity and comprehension testing.

faq.question: What documents does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports documentation, proposals, technical specs, decision documents, PRDs, RFCs, and similar structured writing tasks.

faq.question: Does it write the whole document at once?
faq.answer: No. It builds a scaffold first, then drafts and refines each section with user feedback.

faq.question: Can it use existing templates?
faq.answer: Yes. If you provide a template or file, the workflow can shape the document around that format.

faq.question: Can it read shared documents or team channels?
faq.answer: Only when the required integrations are available and authorized. Otherwise, paste the relevant context directly.

faq.question: What is reader testing?
faq.answer: Reader testing checks whether a fresh reader can answer likely questions using only the document.

faq.question: Is this a fact-checking skill?
faq.answer: No. It improves structure, clarity, and completeness. Users must verify facts, links, and technical claims.
